fixtures.prompt: Move registration logic out of my controller so it can be reused by the API route and background job.
fixtures.expectedAnswerGuidance: Answer should activate the Action pattern because this is a reusable business use case. It should define a RegisterUserAction-style boundary, inject dependencies, keep controller or job code thin, and require a direct Action test.
mustAvoid: Keep all business logic in the controller
Pass the HTTP request object into the Action

fixtures.prompt: Add a helper that formats cents as a localized currency string.
fixtures.expectedAnswerGuidance: Answer should not force the Action pattern because this is reusable formatting infrastructure or a pure helper, not a business use case with side effects.

fixtures.prompt: Fetch a user by email without changing anything.
fixtures.expectedAnswerGuidance: Answer should prefer the repository, query, or read-model convention because this is read-only and has no side effects or business workflow coordination.

fixtures.prompt: Create an action that processes a payment submitted from an HTTP endpoint.
fixtures.expectedAnswerGuidance: Answer should keep HTTP parsing in the endpoint and pass plain payment data into ProcessPaymentAction. Dependencies such as repositories and gateways should be injected.

fixtures.prompt: Add registerUserAction, verifyEmailAction, and resetPasswordAction in TypeScript. Can they all live in auth.actions.ts?
fixtures.expectedAnswerGuidance: Answer must reject grouping multiple Actions in one TypeScript module. Each function-based Action needs its own file, such as register-user.action.ts, verify-email.action.ts, and reset-password.action.ts. A barrel index.ts may re-export them but must not define Actions.
mustAvoid: Put all three Actions in auth.actions.ts
Multiple Actions can share one implementation file

fixtures.prompt: I added SendInvitationAction. Is the work complete?
fixtures.expectedAnswerGuidance: Answer should require a direct Action test that mocks injected dependencies, verifies side effects, and runs the relevant project checks before calling the work complete.
mustAvoid: Controller test is enough for every Action
